Title: Complete pathway elucidation of echinacoside in Cistanche tubulosa and de novo biosynthesis of phenylethanoid glycosides

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes echinacoside and acteoside accumulation by enabling tyramine oxidation in the TyDC-TYO-ADH upstream route to tyrosol.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtTYO1 was the only tested amine oxidase that functioned with CtTyDC and Ct4HPAR in Nicotiana benthamiana to produce tyrosol, and was renamed CtTYO. 2. Together with CtTyDC and Ct4HPAR, CtTYO defines the TyDC-TYO-ADH pathway contributing to echinacoside biosynthesis in Cistanche tubulosa. 3. Combined overexpression of CtTyDC+CtTYO+Ct4HPAR increased echinacoside and acteoside in C. tubulosa suspension cells, whereas CtTyDC+CtTYO RNAi reduced them.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated/coexpressed with downstream ECH genes under 6% PEG6000 in C. tubulosa cell cultures
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 6% PEG6000 drought-mimic treatment in C. tubulosa cell suspension cultures; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome-guided candidate screening; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; overexpression in C. tubulosa suspension cells; RNAi knockdown in C. tubulosa calli; metabolite profiling by HPLC/HRMS
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neuroprotective bioactive compound with relevance to prevention/treatment of neurodegenerative diseases
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In vitro activity of TYO candidates was not detected; function was established in planta using pathway coexpression. CtTYO corresponds to CtTYO1 after functional validation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptome sequencing; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA1175030
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in N. benthamiana transient expression and C. tubulosa overexpression constructs

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes echinacoside biosynthesis by reducing 4-HPAA to tyrosol and 3,4-diHPAA to hydroxytyrosol, the shared terminal step of upstream precursor formation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ct4HPAR reduced 4-HPAA to tyrosol using NADH in vitro. 2. Ct4HPAR also reduced 3,4-diHPAA to hydroxytyrosol, supporting precursor supply to phenylethanoid glycosides. 3. Ct4HPAR functioned with CtTyDC and CtTYO in N. benthamiana and with either pathway 1 or pathway 3 gene combinations to increase echinacoside/acteoside accumulation in C. tubulosa cells.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression pattern correlated with downstream ECH genes in PEG6000-treated C. tubulosa cultures
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 6% PEG6000 treatment; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with NADH; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; overexpression in C. tubulosa suspension cells; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shared last step for both plant TyDC-TYO-ADH and microbial-like TAT-PPDC-ADH routes, and also accepts dihydroxylated aldehyde substrate.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances de novo salidroside production in tobacco by improving glycosyl donor supply.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtSuS was introduced to increase glycosyl donor supplementation during phenylethanoid glycoside biosynthesis. 2. Coexpression of CtSuS with CtTyDC, CtTYO, Ct4HPAR and CtUGT85AF12 increased salidroside yield in tobacco from ~2% DW to ~3% DW. 3. CtSuS supported broader de novo synthesis of multiple PhG derivatives in engineered tobacco.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway coexpression in N. benthamiana and metabolite yield comparison
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive phenolic glycosides; pathway engineering products
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Engineering-support gene rather than a direct dedicated ECH-pathway tailoring enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Downstream glycosylation step forming salidroside and hydroxysalidroside
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early glycosylated intermediates required for ECH biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to tyrosol or hydroxytyrosol to form salidroside or hydroxysalidroside.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early pathway step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of salidroside/hydroxysalidroside, thereby supporting ECH biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtUGT85A191 glucosylated tyrosol to salidroside and hydroxytyrosol to hydroxysalidroside in vitro. 2. Its expression pattern matched PEG6000-induced ECH accumulation in C. tubulosa. 3. It functioned in tobacco de novo synthesis, though CtUGT85AF12 was more active.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression correlated with ECH accumulation in PEG6000-treated C. tubulosa cell cultures
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 6% PEG6000; heterologous expression in E. coli and N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic screening; recombinant protein assay; kinetic assay; NMR product verification; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Higher affinity for tyrosol than hydroxytyrosol; less efficient than CtUGT85AF12 in the heterologous system.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Downstream glycosylation step forming salidroside and hydroxysalidroside
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early glycosylated intermediates required for ECH biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to tyrosol or hydroxytyrosol to form salidroside or hydroxysalidroside.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early pathway step; strongest of the three tested glucosyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes salidroside and hydroxysalidroside formation and enables efficient de novo PhG biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtUGT85AF12 efficiently glucosylated tyrosol and hydroxytyrosol in vitro, with better substrate affinity and catalytic efficiency than CtUGT85A191. 2. Its expression profile matched ECH accumulation under PEG6000. 3. In tobacco, it supported efficient de novo synthesis of salidroside and downstream PhGs.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression correlated with ECH accumulation in PEG6000-treated C. tubulosa cell cultures
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 6% PEG6000; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; kinetics; NMR structure confirmation; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Succulent stem of C. tubulosa; tobacco leaves in heterologous system
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected as preferred glucosyltransferase for heterologous pathway reconstruction due to higher activity.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Can form early intermediates but appears less relevant than the other UGT85s
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to tyrosol or hydroxytyrosol with relatively weak activity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtUGT85AF13 glucosylated tyrosol and hydroxytyrosol in vitro but much more weakly than CtUGT85A191 and CtUGT85AF12. 2. Its expression profile did not match ECH accumulation patterns. 3. It was not prioritized for heterologous pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Did not accord with ECH accumulation pattern in PEG6000-treated cultures
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a functionally active but less decisive core pathway gene because it was characterized in Results.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late downstream sugar-chain elongation / rhamnosylation branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms acteoside from calA and osmB from osmA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose from UDP-rhamnose onto the central glucose of calA or osmA through a rhamnose(1→3)glucose linkage.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key late assembly step; decisive for acteoside formation from calA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes acteoside and osmB production, enabling late ECH pathway assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtUGT79G13 rhamnosylated both osmA and calA, with higher activity toward calA. 2. It converted calA to acteoside, establishing rhamnosylation as the last step of acteoside synthesis in C. tubulosa. 3. Molecular docking suggested structural basis for calA preference. 4. It functioned in N. benthamiana to produce acteoside and additional rhamnosylated PhG derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Candidate selected from transcriptome; activity integrated into in vivo tobacco and Cistanche pathway analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; PEG6000-associated transcriptome screening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay; kinetics; NMR verification of acteoside; AlphaFold2/docking; transient expression and substrate infiltration in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive PhGs contributing to medicinal properties
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: C. tubulosa stem; tobacco leaves in heterologous system
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinct from LrUGT79G7 because it prefers dihydroxylated calA and supports an alternative assembly order in Cistanche.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the 6'-OH position of acteoside to form echinacoside.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtUGT73EV1 specifically recognized acteoside and generated echinacoside in vitro. 2. NMR and MS confirmed the product as ECH. 3. Transient expression with acteoside infiltration, or with CtUGT79G13 plus calA infiltration, produced ECH in N. benthamiana, confirming the last step in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Candidate from transcriptome; function validated in vitro and in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay; kinetic assay; NMR/MS product confirmation; transient expression and substrate infiltration in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Succulent stem; tobacco leaves in heterologous system
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In tobacco, endogenous malonylation may occupy the preferred 6'-OH site and hinder additional glucosylation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Natural 6'-O-glucosyltransferase for final ECH formation reported here.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms osmA and syringalide A, intermediates upstream of acteoside and ECH
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers p-coumaroyl or caffeoyl groups from aromatic acyl-CoAs to salidroside.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of acylated PhG intermediates leading to acteoside and ECH.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among candidate acyltransferases, only CtAT-E acylated salidroside using p-coumaroyl-CoA or caffeoyl-CoA. 2. CtAT-E generated osmA and syringalide A in vitro and in tobacco. 3. Coupling with CtUGT79G13 generated osmB and syringalide A-3'-rhamnoside and reduced acyl migration. 4. CtAT-E supported production of numerous additional PhG derivatives in tobacco.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Candidate selected from PEG6000-upregulated transcriptome genes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 6% PEG6000 transcriptome screen; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics; recombinant enzyme assays; coupled assays with CtUGT79G13; MS-based product identification; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: C. tubulosa stem; tobacco leaves in heterologous system
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Acyl donor degradation and acyl migration products were observed in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accepted endogenous tobacco aromatic acyl-CoAs, enabling diverse non-native PhG analog formation.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Upstream precursor supply for echinacoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies tyramine/dopamine precursor pools for tyrosol/hydroxytyrosol formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates L-tyrosine to tyramine and L-dopa to dopamine.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key committed upstream step in pathway 1; not solely sufficient in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to precursor formation for ECH biosynthesis; together with CtTYO and Ct4HPAR increases ECH and acteoside.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtTyDC was identified as a phenolic amino acid decarboxylase based on sequence determinants and in vitro conversion of L-tyrosine to tyramine. 2. It also converted L-dopa to dopamine. 3. Coexpression with CtTYO and Ct4HPAR generated tyrosol in tobacco. 4. Overexpression/RNAi analyses in Cistanche supported contribution of pathway 1 to ECH biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression profile matched downstream genes and ECH accumulation under PEG6000
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 6% PEG6000; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome analysis; recombinant enzyme assay; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; overexpression and RNAi in C. tubulosa
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single-gene overexpression produced only slight increases in ECH/acteoside, indicating dependence on pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CtTyDCY347F mutant gained aldehyde synthase-like activity, but the native gene functions as decarboxylase.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Upstream precursor supply for echinacoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream entry step of microbial-like branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies 4-HPPA/3,4-diHPPA precursor pool for tyrosol/hydroxytyrosol formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the amino group from tyrosine or dopa to α-ketoglutarate to generate hydroxyphenylpyruvate intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry enzyme of Ehrlich-like pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to ECH precursor supply via the microbial-like Ehrlich pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtTAT catalysed transamination of L-tyrosine to 4-HPPA and L-dopa to 3,4-diHPPA in vitro. 2. Expression correlated with downstream genes under PEG6000. 3. Overexpression and RNAi combinations in C. tubulosa supported a role for the TAT-PPDC-ADH pathway in ECH biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Consistent with identified downstream genes in PEG6000-treated cultures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome-guided screening; recombinant enzyme assay; overexpression and RNAi in C. tubulosa
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No product detected when CtTAT+CtPPDC+Ct4HPAR were transiently expressed in N. benthamiana despite in vitro activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents microbial-like pathway usage in a plant specialized metabolite pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Upstream precursor supply for echinacoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Converts hydroxyphenylpyruvate intermediates into aldehydes used for tyrosol/hydroxytyrosol formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing plant PPDC identified here for Ehrlich-like pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes precursor aldehyde formation for tyrosol/hydroxytyrosol and thereby supports ECH biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtPPDC2 was the only candidate showing PPDC activity and was renamed CtPPDC. 2. It converted 4-HPPA to 4-HPAA and 3,4-diHPPA to 3,4-diHPAA in vitro. 3. Together with CtTAT and Ct4HPAR, it defines a microbial-like Ehrlich pathway contributing to ECH biosynthesis in Cistanche.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: CtPPDC2 and CtPPDC3 increased under PEG6000, but only CtPPDC2 was functional
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate screening; recombinant enzyme assay with NaBH4-coupled product capture; overexpression and RNAi in C. tubulosa
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No product detected in N. benthamiana coexpression despite in vitro activity of the Ehrlich pathway enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First plant PPDC for this pathway identified in this system.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydroxylation step producing calA before rhamnosylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms calA, the preferred substrate of CtUGT79G13 en route to acteoside and ECH
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Likely osmA/syringalide-type intermediate context in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Calceolarioside A; related hydroxylated intermediates including compound 17/19 context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses hydroxylation introducing the phenolic C3 hydroxy group in the downstream ECH pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Weak catalytic step and current bottleneck in heterologous ECH production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables hydroxylated intermediates required for acteoside and echinacoside formation, but with weak activity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtCYP98A248 was identified among oxidase/hydroxylase candidates and phylogenetically grouped with CYP98A family enzymes. 2. Coexpression in N. benthamiana generated trace calA and related hydroxylated products. 3. Results indicated that C3 hydroxylation occurs before rhamnosylation in C. tubulosa. 4. Weak activity was recognized as a limitation for efficient ECH reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate screening; pathway coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS detection of trace hydroxylated products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Succulent stem; tobacco leaves in heterologous system
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Weak activity limited ECH yield in heterologous production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Further activity improvement was stated to be under investigation.
